title: Forced to Wear a Burqa in Kabul: My Life Just Flipped! 😭 #shorts #storytime
script: Mandatory burqa rules in Kabul: my life just flipped upside down.  I'm in Kabul, and they're making us wear burqas every time we step outside. My family's arguing nonstop about it. My mom says it's for our safety, but I feel like I'm disappearing. At school, my friends and I whisper about how unfair it is, but we're all scared. I mean, what if we get caught without one?   Every morning, I stand in front of the mirror, looking at the burqa, feeling trapped. My dad says we have to follow the rules, but I can't shake this feeling of defiance. It's like my identity is being erased. I hear stories of girls getting in trouble, and it terrifies me.   One day, I decide enough is enough. I walk to school without it, heart pounding, eyes scanning for trouble. --I feel eyes on me--, and I know I'm ta

title: Is Cultural Sharing a Crime Now? 😭 Let's Embrace Traditions! #shorts #muslim
script: Why is it that sharing cultures is seen as a crime now? Wallah, it’s just respect and love, not appropriation!  I’m seriously confused, bro. Like, why do people think cultural sharing is some kind of crime? My friends, who aren’t even Muslim, join me for iftar during Ramadan. They show up, break fast with me, and it’s all about solidarity and respect. But some people are out here calling it appropriation? Astaghfirullah, that’s just ridiculous!  And get this, my black friends used to braid my hair. We were celebrating their culture, and it was awesome! But now, people act like appreciating someone else’s traditions is a bad thing. Why can’t we just enjoy and learn from each other without all this drama?  Culture is meant to be shared, habibi. It’s beautiful when we embrace each other’s tra
